title: Detect Consecutive Orders
description: Find orders where the difference between current and previous order date for a customer is exactly one day.
sql_solution: SELECT customer_id, date FROM (SELECT *, LAG(date) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY date) prev_date FROM orders) WHERE DATE(date) = DATE(prev_date, '+1 day');
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

order_window = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(to_date(col("date")))

result = (
    orders.withColumn("prev_date", lag("date").over(order_window))
    .filter(datediff(to_date(col("date")), to_date(col("prev_date"))) == 1)
    .select("customer_id", "date")
)
